content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
November 16,
1993
Joe Velasquez
City Councilmember
City of Cathedral City
P.O.
Box 5001
Cathedral City,
CA 92235
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1—93—299
Dear COuncilmember Velasquez:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice
regarding your responsibilities under the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
Since your
advice request does not refer to a specific pending governmental
decision,
we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance.
2
QUESTION
May you participate in city council decisions concerning the
layoff of the former Senior Building Inspector?
CONCLUSION
From the facts you have provided, it would appear that the
former Senior Building Inspector is not a “source of income” to
you.
Consequently, absent some other disqualifying economic
interest as set forth in Section 87103,
or some direct financial
effect on you personally resulting from city council decisions
involving the layoff of the former Senior Building Inspector,
you
may participate in such decisions.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000—18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
inuuunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section
83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807
•
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
1—93—299
Page
2
FACTS
You are a city council member of the City of Cathedral City.
The city council may consider providing a hearing to a city
employee who has been laid off as a result of action taken by the
city council to abolish the Senior Building Inspector position,
rather than the Senior Plans Examiner position,
in the Building
Division.
The employee who filled the position of Senior Building
Inspector position believes that you should not participate in any
city council decision concerning his layoff since he has inspected
projects in which you and your masonry trade business
(a
partnership in which you have a
50% ownership interest)
have been
involved.
The employee contends that you have a conflict of
interest, since on one recent project, he did not approve your job
on his first visit and he had to come back for reinspection.
You
claim that this action resulted in no financial cost to you or
your partnership.
However,
you acknowledge that if the city
decided not to lay off the Senior Building Inspector,
you would
reconsider conducting business in the city.
Last year you did
approximately $100,000 of total business in the city.
In your letter,
you state that the city decidedto abolish
the position of Senior Building Inspector, rather than the Senior
Plans Examiner,
for reasons unrelated to the inspection by the
Senior Building Inspector on your projects.
Specifically,
you
state that a large number of building contractors spoke to the
city council in favor of retaining the Senior Plans Examiner
position because of his outstanding service to business people,
especially the Hispanic citizens of the city.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in making,
or otherwise using his or her official
position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest.
Section 87103 specifies that
an official has a financial interest if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public generally,
on the official or a member of his or her immediate family or on:
(a)
Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.
(b)
Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.


Our File No.
1—93—299
Page
3
(c)
Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a connuercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status,
aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars
($250)
or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(d)
Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.
(e)
Any donor of,
or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of,
a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars
($250)
or more in value
provided to, received by,
or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.
Section 87103.
From the facts you have provided,
you have not eceived any
income from the former Senior Building Inspector.
Thus, under the
Act, the former Senior Building Inspector is not a “source of
income” to you.
Consequently, absent some other disqualifying economic
interest as set forth in Section 87103, or some direct financial
effect on you personally resulting from city council decisions
involving the layoff of the former Senior Building Inspector,
you
may participate in such decisions.
Income of an individual also includes a pro—rata share of any
income to any business entity or trust in which the individual or
spouse owns, directly, indirectly or beneficially,
a 10—percent
interest or greater.
(Section 82030.)
For example, if an
official has a 50—percent interest in a business, those persons
that paid an official’s business $500 or more in the past
12
months are sources of income of $250 to the official.
Thus,
you
may not participate in any city council decisions involving the
layoff of the former Senior Building Inspector if the decisions
would have a material financial effect on your masonry contracting
business or on any clients of your masonry business.


Our File No.
1-93—299
Page
4
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested.
If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322—59Ol.
Sincerely,
Steven C.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Deanne Stone
Senior Commission Counsel
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively,
copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of lO per page.

you

inuuunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.

Our File No.
1—93—299
